About the Business

Who is Alchemy Underwriting?

Alchemy Underwriting was established in 2023 by seasoned London Market insurance experts, bringing together deep underwriting expertise with a clear ambition: to build an agency that delivers genuine excellence through enduring relationships built on trust.

As a trusted Lloyd's Coverholder backed by A-rated carriers, Alchemy focuses on rigorous risk selection, innovative underwriting, and advanced risk modelling across Global Property, Energy, Professional Indemnity, Marine, and other specialist lines. They are a dynamic, growing business with a clear sense of purpose and a reputation that is already making waves in the Lloyd's market.

The claims team sits at the heart of Alchemy's proposition. With 11 claims professionals across the business, 7 of whom sit within the Property team, you would be joining the core part of what they do. This is a business where claims is not an afterthought; it is a genuine point of competitive difference and a source of real pride.

Trust

Their clients can always count on them to deliver.

Integrity

Strong principles sit at the core of everything they do.

Boldness

They innovate and take calculated, well-reasoned risks.

Communication

Honest, approachable and responsive at every level.

The Opportunity

The Role

This is a genuine opportunity to build your claims career in one of the most exciting young businesses in the Lloyd's market. As Claims Adjuster, you will be responsible for the efficient review and settlement of claims across the AUL Claims division, working primarily on first party Open Market Property and Upstream Energy risks.

You will manage your own caseload from first notification through to settlement, maintaining high standards of file management and ensuring that both brokers and capacity providers are kept informed and well-served throughout. The role carries real variety and, as the business continues to grow, there is a clear path to broadening your experience into other lines including Contingency, Aviation, Marine, and PV/Terrorism.

You will report to Chris Webber, Claims Manager, and work closely with the wider claims community to deliver a market-leading service. Alchemy takes claims seriously as a differentiator, and this is a role where your contribution will be visible and valued from day one.

Division AUL Claims
Reports To Chris Webber, Claims Manager
Primary Lines Open Market Property & Upstream Energy
Team Size 11 claims professionals, 7 in Property
Office Days 3 days per week, Tuesday as core day
Location 2 Seething Lane, London EC3N 4AT
Salary Salary on Application
Day to Day

What You'll Be Doing

The role spans three core areas, each equally important to Alchemy's reputation for exceptional claims service.

Claims Management

  • Proactive adjustment and settlement of claims from first notification to close
  • Maintaining complete, well-structured claims files throughout
  • Delivering a premier service for all clients and brokers
  • Working primarily on Open Market Property and Upstream Energy risks
  • Opportunity to develop experience across other classes as the business grows

External Relationships

  • Working closely with capital providers to keep them well informed
  • Managing broker expectations clearly and proactively
  • Ensuring clients are updated throughout the claims process
  • Promoting Alchemy's reputation in every external interaction
  • Contributing to market-leading standards of claims service

Market Awareness

  • Proactively staying current with market and industry developments
  • Networking with the wider claims community
  • Attending relevant conferences and briefings
  • Recording Continuous Professional Development (CPD) throughout
  • Supporting Alchemy's ambition to have a best-in-class claims offering
What We're Looking For

Requirements & Competencies

You do not need to tick every box. This is a role built for someone early in their career who is eager to learn, take ownership, and grow within a supportive and ambitious team.

Experience & Education

  • A couple of years' experience in insurance claims, ideally in the Lloyd's or London Market
  • University degree or equivalent (desirable, not essential)
  • Insurance professional qualification (desirable, not essential)
  • Practical, hands-on experience handling claims files
  • Some exposure to property, energy, or specialty lines beneficial

Core Competencies

  • Comfortable working collaboratively within a team environment
  • Strong communication skills, written and verbal
  • Genuine drive to deliver results and take ownership of your workload
  • Understanding of London and international market structure and practices
  • Commercial awareness and a desire to understand the wider business context
What to Expect

The Interview Process

We believe in a transparent, straightforward process. Here is exactly what to expect, step by step.

1
Screening Call with Erik
A relaxed introductory call with your consultant at Claims Recruitment Services. We will walk through your background, answer your questions, and explore whether this feels like the right move for you.
2
1st Interview with Alchemy
A meet and greet with Chris Webber, Claims Manager. Expect a friendly conversation covering your CV, your background in claims, and a chance to hear more about the role and the team firsthand.
3
2nd Interview with Alchemy
A slightly more in-depth conversation with the Head of Claims. The focus here is culture fit and understanding how you would approach the role. It is semi-formal and conversational in style.
4
Offer & Onboarding
If both sides are keen to move forward, we will work with you and Alchemy to agree terms and make it happen as smoothly as possible.

Preparing Well: A Few Pointers

◆ Spend some time on Alchemy's website. Understand what they write, who their clients are, and what sets them apart from larger, more traditional Lloyd's players.
◆ Be ready to talk through a claim you have handled end to end. Walk the interviewer through your approach, the challenges you faced, and what you learnt.
◆ Think about why you want to move at this point in your career. Alchemy is a young, growing business. Interviewers will be interested in your appetite for that kind of environment.
◆ Come with questions. The second interview in particular is as much about you assessing them as it is the reverse. Genuine curiosity goes a long way.
Package & Benefits

What Alchemy Offers

A competitive package that reflects how seriously Alchemy takes looking after its people.

🌞
30 Days Annual Leave
Generous holiday entitlement well above the market standard, giving you the time to switch off and recharge properly.
🆕
9% Employer Pension
A substantial employer pension contribution of 9%, helping you build long-term financial security from day one.
💓
8x Death in Service
Life assurance at 8x your basic annual salary, providing meaningful protection for you and your family.
🏥
Private Medical Insurance
Full private medical cover extended to your dependants too, provided as a benefit in kind on payroll.
🏢
Hybrid Working
3 days per week in the office with Tuesday as the core team day, giving you genuine flexibility alongside real team connection.
🌟
Career Development
A clear path to broaden your experience across multiple classes as the business grows, with CPD actively supported and encouraged.
Where You'll Be Based

London Office

Alchemy's London trading office sits in the heart of the City, steps from the Lloyd's building and the wider insurance market.
